detail: Merged Greene Holcomb & Fisher LLC / GHF with the post-acquisition label BMO Capital Markets Corp. into a single advisor actor because the filing expressly states that BMO acquired GHF’s business and refers to the same advisor as GHF before and BMO after the acquisition.
Classified the Board as target_board because it is the Company’s governing body supervising the sale process and approving the merger.
Classified the Board Subcommittee as target_board because it is a committee of independent directors tasked with interviewing bankers and evaluating strategic alternatives.
Classified the Transaction Committee as target_board because it is the special committee of independent directors overseeing the sale process on the Board’s behalf.
Classified GHF / BMO as advisor rather than bidder because the filing describes it as the Company’s retained investment banking firm running the outreach process.
Classified Hinckley, Allen & Snyder LLP as advisor because the filing identifies it as the Company’s legal counsel participating in Transaction Committee meetings.
Classified Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP as advisor although it was retained by bidder G&W, because it appears in the chronology as legal counsel rather than as an acquisition counterparty.
Classified Party A as a strategic bidder because the filing identifies it as a Class I rail partner that expressed interest in acquiring equity in the Company.
Classified G&W as a strategic bidder because the filing places it among potential strategic buyers and it ultimately executed the merger agreement.
Classified Party B as a strategic bidder because the filing expressly introduces it as another potential strategic buyer.
Classified Party C as a strategic bidder because the filing expressly introduces it as a potential strategic buyer that approached management in early July 2016.
Classified Party D as a financial bidder because the filing expressly labels Party D as a financial buyer.
Classified Party E as a strategic bidder because the filing expressly labels Party E as a strategic buyer.
Classified Party F as a strategic bidder because the filing expressly labels Party F as a strategic buyer even though it later provided financing support for Party E’s revised LOI.

Applied bidder/strategic classification to unnamed_1 through unnamed_6 because the filing reports 11 strategic buyers under confidentiality agreements, while the bookmarked chronology separately names Party A, G&W, Party B, Party E, and Party F from that cohort; Party C is tracked separately as a later entrant.

Applied bidder/financial classification to unnamed_7 through unnamed_19 because the filing reports 14 financial buyers under confidentiality agreements, while Party D is the only individually labeled financial buyer in the bookmarked chronology.
Recorded total parties_contacted = 29 from the filing’s 11 strategic + 18 financial split while also preserving the strategic and financial subcounts as separate assertions.
Recorded total nda_signed = 25 from the filing’s 11 strategic + 14 financial NDA split while also preserving the strategic and financial subcounts as separate assertions.
Recorded total bidders_declined_loi = 2 from the filing’s one strategic buyer + one financial buyer split while also preserving the subgroup counts as separate assertions.

Minted unnamed_1 through unnamed_19 from the 25-party NDA cohort (6 strategic, 13 financial) after separately tracking Party A, G&W, Party B, Party D, Party E, and Party F; did not separately mint the four additional contacted-only financial buyers from the 18-contacted / 14-NDA gap because Skill 4 is an initial fail-open roster rather than a full participation census.

Did not mint GATX as a bidder actor because the chronology mentions it only as Director Smith’s affiliated company and a potential conflict source, not as a contacted or participating sale-process counterparty.
Did not mint the Company’s unnamed STB counsel as a separate advisor actor because the chronology identifies only the role, not the firm name.
